sources: Avoma: Cadence Design Discovery w/ Zycus, 2026-02-25
metrics.narrative: Devesh Chadha named target outcomes on the 25 Feb 2026 discovery call - a seamless end-to-end platform serving procurement, legal, and sales, efficient transaction throughput, AI-driven agreement creation, and strict infosec compliance. But a core pricing input, annual contract volume, remained unresolved as of the 13 Apr 2026 pricing call, where Jason Lieu cited an internal estimate from Melanie Hamm of roughly 12,500 contracts/year against Zycus's initial 500-1,000 assumption, and no quantified ROI case has been confirmed with anyone above evaluator level since.

sources: Avoma: Intro Call | Cadence Design x Zycus, 2026-01-23
champion.narrative: Peter Hughes (Senior Category Manager, Evaluator) is the most consistently engaged contact - driving workshop scheduling, POC timing, and coordination with the Zycus account team through 29 Jun 2026 - but holds no confirmed internal advocacy role or budget influence. Legal's own preference leaned toward Ironclad from the outset (23 Jan 2026), a preference never countered by any later evidence, leaving Peter Hughes' engagement developing rather than a secured, powerful champion.

sources: Avoma: Intro Call | Cadence Design x Zycus, 2026-01-23
competition.narrative: Cadence is confirmed down to a two-vendor final round: Ironclad, which legal's team was 'impressed by' as early as 23 Jan 2026, and Zycus, per the 9 Jun 2026 transition note ('Down to 1 of 2 vendors including Ironclad'). Third-party intake tools (referred to as 'Tonkin' and 'Yep') were evaluated and rejected on cost/capability grounds on 23 Jan 2026. Coupa is the buyer's current tool for CLM and procurement spend management but is not confirmed as a remaining finalist for this deal.

sources: Avoma: Cadence Design Discovery w/ Zycus, 2026-02-25
identify_pain.narrative: Devesh Chadha named the core pain on the 25 Feb 2026 discovery call: manual, disparate contract intake split across Salesforce and email, no standardized metadata capture across buy-side vendor and sell-side legal/sales contracts, and renewal metrics tracked manually by the sourcing team - all driving the search for one unifying platform.

sources: Avoma: Zycus CLM Workshop - Follow Up Session, 2026-04-21
paper_process.narrative: The MSA was submitted 21 Apr 2026 (logged completed), and the same-day workshop recorded a commitment to send the Zycus master software agreement to Cadence legal 'immediately to kick off parallel legal review and avoid bottlenecks.' No touchpoint or Next Step entry since shows any redline, SOW, or further legal movement - over ten weeks of silence on paper despite a close date now one day past due.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget approval sits with unnamed 'Cadence leadership,' referenced in the 26 May 2026 Next Step note as convening 'this week to listen to final budget requests,' but no economic buyer has ever appeared on a Zycus call. Devesh Chadha (Director, Procurement COE) is the most senior contact engaged directly, but has not been referenced in any interaction since 28 Apr 2026, and there is no evidence the ROI case has been walked through with whoever actually controls the budget.

sources: Avoma: Zycus Proposed CLM POC Framework Review for Cadence Design, 2026-04-28
decision_process.narrative: The RFP process began in fall 2025 per the 25 Feb 2026 discovery call, moving through demos, a two-day-then-extended sandbox, and a full POC (kicked off 12 May 2026 with daily reviews). A Cadence leadership budget-review meeting was slated for the week of 26 May 2026 with vendor selection expected 'first week of June,' but a 9 Jun 2026 transition note confirms the decision slipped instead to 'mid-July,' with Cadence down to one of two finalists including Ironclad.

sources: Avoma: Cadence Design Discovery w/ Zycus, 2026-02-25

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria were laid out concretely across two sessions: the 25 Feb 2026 discovery call named a single platform for procurement/legal/sales, throughput, AI-driven agreement creation, and infosec compliance as top success metrics, and the 21 Apr 2026 CLM use-case workshop with Melanie Hamm specified detailed functional requirements - conditional coded-template clause logic, sales-finance restricted access to signed documents only, Salesforce-triggered contract initiation, and DocuSign integration.

items: No Zycus-Cadence call recorded since 2026-04-28 (~72 days); three May touchpoints were cancelled or not recorded.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is cooling: no Zycus-Cadence call has been read in ~72 days, the decision has already slipped once, and the economic buyer has never engaged, even though a buyer-initiated connection and email reply in mid-to-late June show the relationship is not dead.

north_star_verdict.math: Today is 2026-07-08. Close date 2026-07-10 is 2 days out. Deal created 2026-01-23 (~166 days ago), still at Shortlisted (has not reached Vendor Selected/Negotiation). Last live buyer working session read was 2026-04-28 (~71 days ago); last confirmed two-way buyer touch was an inbound email reply on 2026-06-19 (~19 days ago). Vendor decision originally expected end of April, then early June (per 26 May note), now pushed to mid-July (per 9 Jun note) — one confirmed slip already, with 2 days left before the recorded close date and no evidence of contracting (MSA sent 21 Apr 2026 is the only paper-process artifact).
evidence: 9 Jun 2026 Next Step: 'Decision pushed out until mid-July. Down to 1 of 2 vendors including Ironclad.'
26 May 2026 Next Step: vendor selection anticipated first week of June — did not occur as planned.
21 Apr 2026: MSA submitted (only paper-process step to date); no redlines, SOW, or legal review evidenced since.
north_star_verdict.headline: Cadence named Zycus one of a final two (with Ironclad) on 9 Jun 2026, but the internal budget/vendor-selection decision that was due early June has slipped to mid-July, the Zycus account changed owners (Kevin Cipollaro to Karson Keogh, ~9 Jun 2026) with no confirmed live buyer session since, and the close date of 10 Jul 2026 (2 days from today) has no supporting contracting activity behind it.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (or push Close Date to align with the buyer's stated mid-July vendor-selection decision plus a realistic 3-6 week contracting window)

flags: Ironclad remains a live, credible alternative with an unresolved early legal preference (Devesh Chadha, 23 Jan); pair Melanie Hamm with a Zycus legal-ops reference customer before the mid-July vote to neutralize it.

No economic buyer engaged. Budget approval sits with unnamed 'Cadence leadership' who has never spoken to the relevant stakeholder and ROI never socialized to CFO or budget owner, leaving commercials undefended in final decision.
No champion with EB access or advocacy. Peter Hughes (Evaluator) is a coordinator, not an advocate. Devesh Chadha (Decision Maker) newly engaged, passive, no evidence of push for Zycus.

No champion. Peter Hughes (procurement project lead, Evaluator contact role) is closest candidate but has not demonstrated advocacy. His last call was 21 Apr; since then referenced only in third person in Next_Step. No one actively selling Zycus internally or countering legal's Ironclad preference.

Close date 10 Jul is 2 days out; decision pushed to mid-July (9 Jun Next_Step). No contracting activity since MSA submission 21 Apr (no redlines, no SOW, no PO). Minimum 1-week post-decision slip to signature, realistic 3-4 weeks for full legal review. Close will slip to late Jul or early Aug.
Best Case forecast with no EB engagement, no champion, live Ironclad competition, and decision not yet made is indefensible. Recommended forecast: Pipeline.

The early-June rep handoff risks losing the momentum built across six months of workshops; the new deal owner should personally reconnect with Peter Hughes and Devesh Chadha now rather than rely on the Palo Alto event invite alone.

The account changed owners mid-cycle (Kevin Cipollaro to Karson Keogh, ~9 Jun 2026) with no confirmed live session since; rebuild continuity with Peter Hughes now, using the Palo Alto event as the reconnect vehicle, before the final-two decision is made without a de facto Zycus voice in the room.
best_practice_check.summary: The two highest-leverage moves are closing the access-to-power gap and directly countering legal's early lean toward Ironclad before the two-vendor decision finalizes.

customer_preference.evidence: Peter Hughes described Cadence as running a parallel sandbox with 'the other candidate solution' (Ironclad) with equal footing and no preference voiced either way, 28 Apr 2026 (Zycus Proposed CLM POC Framework Review).

competitive_position.summary: Cadence has narrowed its CLM search to two finalists - Zycus and Ironclad - with legal's early, unaddressed preference for Ironclad the single biggest live threat; Coupa remains the incumbent tool for adjacent procurement/CLM functions but is not confirmed as an active bake-off participant, and earlier third-party intake tools were rejected outright.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A technically deep, multi-functional evaluation (discovery through a full May POC) sits alongside three unresolved structural gaps - no economic buyer engagement, an unaddressed legal preference for Ironclad, and a stale close date with no recent contracting motion - that together cap how confidently this can be called a win right now.

deal_risk.text: Close-date credibility: the 10 Jul 2026 close is one day out with the buyer's own target now 'mid-July' and zero visible contracting motion since 21 Apr 2026.
Competitive risk: Ironclad remains a live finalist with legal's unaddressed early preference for it.
Access-to-power risk: the actual budget decision-maker has never been on a Zycus call.

win_position.text: No economic buyer has ever joined a Zycus call - budget approval sits with unnamed 'Cadence leadership' per the 26 May 2026 Next Step note, and nothing since confirms that authority favors Zycus over Ironclad.

Legal's early preference for Ironclad ('impressed by tools like iClad,' 23 Jan 2026) has never been directly countered, and Cadence is confirmed down to 1 of 2 finalists including Ironclad as of 9 Jun 2026.

Deep multi-functional engagement occurred through the evaluation - discovery, demos, InfoSec/architecture review, a full POC from 12 May 2026, and detailed pricing/legal workshops - giving Zycus real technical and commercial standing among the final two.

No Zycus-Cadence call has been read since 28 Apr 2026 (~72 days); three subsequent May touchpoints were cancelled or not recorded, and the only confirmed contact since is a buyer email reply on 19 Jun 2026 and Next Step notes through 29 Jun 2026.

deal_momentum.text: The recorded close date (10 Jul 2026) is one day away, but the buyer's own 9 Jun 2026 note pushed the decision to 'mid-July,' meaning the Salesforce date is already stale.
A buyer-initiated connection occurred on 16 Jun 2026 ('asked for time to connect and invited to Palo Alto event'), a real two-way signal roughly three weeks ago.
No paper-process progress (redlines, SOW) has been logged since the MSA was submitted on 21 Apr 2026, despite a same-day commitment to fast-track parallel legal review.

customer_commitment.text: Cadence ran a full POC (kicked off 12 May 2026 with daily reviews) and secured a second reference customer (completed 28 Apr 2026), both real buyer-side investments of time and effort.
Sales finance flagged the CLM rollout as added workload during the 21 Apr 2026 workshop, and the requested restricted-access model (signed docs and FAQ only) remains unresolved.
